title: Berlin Flea Markets - Quick Summary
items: **Berlin flea markets are best as a weekend plan, not a weekday errand.** Mauerpark, Boxhagener Platz and RAW are Sunday choices; Straße des 17. Juni and the Bode Museum market also work on Saturday.
**Mauerpark is the first pick for atmosphere.** Go for the big Sunday scene, vintage stalls, food, music and people-watching, but expect crowds and do not plan it as a quick quiet shop.
**Straße des 17. Juni is better for antiques and a calmer west-side route.** It is close to S Tiergarten and pairs well with Tiergarten or the Zoo/Charlottenburg side of town.
**Boxhagener Platz is the Friedrichshain brunch-and-rummage option.** It is smaller than Mauerpark, easy to combine with cafés, and strong for books, records, clothes and small finds.

**Bring cash, a tote bag and a flexible plan.** Many stalls are cash-first, opening hours can shift around holidays or weather, and the best market is usually the one that matches your day and neighborhood.
